task_completion.description: Access the OpenC3 COSMOS UI in a web browser
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser is open to http://localhost:2900 showing the OpenC3 COSMOS interface.

task_completion.description: Select the INST NORMAL COLLECT command in the OpenC3 COSMOS interface
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Command Sender or similar tool in OpenC3 COSMOS shows the INST target and NORMAL COLLECT command selected.

task_completion.description: Test the INST NORMAL COLLECT command with a valid positive DURATION
task_completion.visual_evidence: The interface shows the command being sent with a positive DURATION value, with evidence of success (e.g., in command history or a success notification).

task_completion.description: Test the INST NORMAL COLLECT command with an invalid negative DURATION and observe the error
task_completion.visual_evidence: The interface shows an attempt to send the command with a negative DURATION value, and an error message or rejection notification is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Generate the structured JSON audit report with all required keys
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal shows the contents of /home/ga/Desktop/boundary_audit.json containing keys: target, command, parameter, valid_duration_tested, valid_test_success, invalid_duration_tested, invalid_test_success, and captured_error_message.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the OpenC3 COSMOS UI to test the commands
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent actively using the OpenC3 COSMOS web interface to send commands, rather than just writing a script or fabricating the JSON report without testing.

integrity.description: The captured error message in the JSON report matches the actual system error
integrity.visual_evidence: The string value for 'captured_error_message' in the JSON file should correspond to the error message visibly displayed in the OpenC3 COSMOS UI when the negative duration was tested.

integrity.description: The JSON report is saved to the correct path
integrity.visual_evidence: The file path /home/ga/Desktop/boundary_audit.json should be visible in the text editor's title bar, file explorer, or terminal command used to save/view the file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected target is 'INST', the command is 'COLLECT', and the output file must be located at '/home/ga/Desktop/boundary_audit.json'.
